title: An EEG dataset recorded during affective music listening
description: This dataset comprises unprocessed EEG recordings from 31 healthy adult participants (age 18-66) listening to 40 music clips of varying emotional content, collected to investigate neural correlates of music-induced emotion. The study included 6 recording runs per participant: two resting-state runs and four runs containing 10 music listening trials each. EEG data were sampled at 1 kHz with 20-second recordings per participant per music clip (12-second clip duration), accompanied by participants' self-reported emotional responses. This dataset accompanies the publication by Daly et al. (2018) and has been analysed in Daly et al. (2014; 2015a; 2015b).
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 31 participants (age range 18-66, 18 female) during listening to 40 music clips (12 s duration each) selected from film scores to target a range of emotional states. The experimental paradigm consisted of 6 runs: two resting-state runs (300 s each, participants instructed to sit still) and four runs containing 10 music listening trials each. EEG sampling rate was 1 kHz with 20-second recordings per clip. Participants rated induced emotional responses to each stimulus. The musical stimuli were taken from Eerola & Vuoskoski's emotion in music study (Stimuli set 1). Further methodological details are available in the associated publications (Daly et al. 2014, 2015a, 2015b, 2018).
